solution_summary: Implement the cancellation workflow for bookings by verifying them, cancelling as per the logic defined in booking_service.py, and clearly communicating the refund outcome to the user.
implementation_steps: Update the bookings.py file to handle booking cancellation requests and ensure verification logic is in place.
Enhance booking_service.py to include cancellation and refund processes along with appropriate error handling and messaging.
Add unit tests to cover edge cases related to booking cancellation to ensure robust functionality.

planned_changes.change_summary: Implement the API route for booking cancellation, verify booking status, and return appropriate response messages.
planned_changes.rationale: This file manages the API requests for booking actions, so modifications here will allow for handling cancellations.

planned_changes.change_summary: Add cancellation and refund logic, including error handling for invalid requests and race conditions.
planned_changes.rationale: The business logic for handling cancellations and refunds should be encapsulated in the service layer to maintain clean architecture.

patch_strategy: Create a feature branch, make necessary modifications to the selected files, and ensure the new functionality is unit tested before merging into the main branch.
validation_steps: Run unit tests specifically for the booking cancellation workflow to validate functionality.
Perform manual testing on the cancellation API endpoint to ensure it behaves as expected in various scenarios.
Check logs and responses for any errors during the cancellation process, ensuring clear messaging is provided to the user.
pr_body: This PR implements the cancellation process for bookings, allowing customers to cancel their bookings for a refund. It includes verification of the booking status, invokes refund processes, and provides clear communications regarding the refund outcome. Unit tests for edge cases have also been added.
risks: Potential race conditions in booking status updates during concurrent modification.
Errors in user messaging if cancellation statuses are not handled correctly.
assumptions: Booking records have a defined status that can be queried before cancellation.
Communication channels are in place to inform users about refund outcomes.
